title: Bitter Australian Cheddar Bake
description: A crispy, comforting side dish that's perfect for a lazy Sunday. Featuring mature cheddar and a sweet and spicy kick from ferments.
instructions: Mix together the flour, cold butter and egg replacer until a crumbly dough forms.
In a separate bowl, combine the cheese, ferment jang, salt, black pepper and vegan Worcestershire sauce.
Add the chopped chives and parsley to the cheese mixture and stir to combine.
Roll out the dough on a floured surface to a thickness of 1cm (0.5in).
Transfer the dough to a baking tray lined with parchment paper, leaving a 1cm (0.5in) border around the edges.
Spread the cheese mixture evenly over the dough, leaving a 1cm (0.5in) border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the dough up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is hot and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5-10 minutes before serving.
